text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY CLINIC - FOLLOW-UP NOTE

ATTENDING: [Attending Oncologist]
DIVISION: Thoracic Oncology
VISIT TYPE: Established Patient Follow-Up / End-of-Doublet Assessment

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Ms. Margaret Yuen is a 64-year-old Chinese-American woman with Stage IIIB (T3N3M0) EGFR L858R-mutant left upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma presenting today for oncology assessment after completing 6 cycles of carboplatin/pemetrexed chemotherapy. The primary goals of today's visit are to review restaging results, address ongoing toxicities, and discuss the transition plan including clinical trial options.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Yuen's oncologic history is well established. She was initially diagnosed with Stage IIIB EGFR L858R-mutant NSCLC adenocarcinoma approximately 2 years ago, treated with definitive concurrent carboplatin/paclitaxel chemoradiation (60 Gy in 30 fractions) followed by consolidation osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily per the LAURA trial paradigm. She had a sustained partial response lasting approximately 14 months before developing systemic and intracranial progression -- a 2.1 cm left upper lobe lesion (increased from 1.3 cm), a new 0.9 cm right lower lobe nodule, and a right adrenal nodule on CT, along with a 1.4 cm right frontal lobe brain metastasis and a 0.7 cm left cerebellar metastasis on MRI brain.

Biopsy of the right lower lobe nodule at progression confirmed maintained adenocarcinoma histology (no small cell transformation), retained L858R, and acquired T790M resistance mutation. Comprehensive NGS also revealed high-level MET amplification (copy number ratio 5.8, up from 2.4 at diagnosis), a PIK3CA E545K co-mutation, and retained STK11/KEAP1 co-mutations. Given the limited brain metastases, she underwent stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) to both lesions -- 20 Gy x1 to the right frontal and 22 Gy x1 to the left cerebellar lesion. She then initiated second-line systemic therapy with carboplatin AUC 5 / pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 every 21 days, completing 6 cycles over approximately 4.5 months.

Today's visit marks the end of the doublet phase. The most recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (reviewed below) showed stable to minor improvement -- the left upper lobe lesion measures 1.8 x 1.4 cm (down from 2.1 x 1.7 cm), the right lower lobe nodule shrank from 0.9 to 0.5 cm, and the right adrenal nodule is stable/slightly smaller at 0.6 cm. Brain MRI from 3 months post-SRS demonstrated excellent intracranial response: the right frontal site shows post-treatment necrotic change (0.9 cm, central necrosis with rim enhancement) and the left cerebellar lesion has resolved completely. No new intracranial lesions.

Over the course of carboplatin/pemetrexed, she experienced Grade 2 nausea (well managed with scheduled ondansetron and prochlorperazine), Grade 2 fatigue, Grade 1 alopecia, Grade 1 thrombocytopenia, and Grade 2 anemia requiring erythropoiesis-stimulating agent support and a single unit packed RBC transfusion after cycle 3. Her ECOG PS transiently worsened to 2 during cycles 2-3 but recovered to PS 1 by cycle 4 and has remained 1 since.

Today, she reports:
- Persistent but improved fatigue (Grade 1 currently, much better than mid-treatment)
- Nausea essentially resolved since completing carboplatin
- No hemoptysis, no dyspnea at rest, mild exertional dyspnea on significant exertion (likely multifactorial -- post-radiation fibrosis, deconditioning)
- No headache, no visual changes, no focal neurological symptoms, no seizures
- No chest pain
- Appetite has improved; has regained approximately 2 lbs over the past 4 weeks
- She denies lower extremity edema at this time
- Bowel function normalizing; no diarrhea currently

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive: mild fatigue (Grade 1), mild exertional dyspnea, hair thinning (ongoing but improving). Negative: fevers/chills, night sweats, chest pain, hemoptysis, cough (mild residual dry cough, chronic from prior radiation), syncope, headache, diplopia, focal weakness, numbness/tingling, dysphagia, nausea/vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain, hematuria, new rashes, palpitations, lower extremity swelling.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY / ONCOLOGIC HISTORY (Summarized):
- Stage IIIB (T3N3M0) left upper lobe adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R, STK11/KEAP1 co-mutant; PD-L1 TPS 15%
- Treated: concurrent carboplatin/paclitaxel + thoracic RT (60 Gy/30 fx), then osimertinib maintenance x ~14 months
- Progression with T790M resistance, MET amplification (high-level), new brain metastases
- SRS x2 lesions (right frontal, left cerebellar); now 3 months post-SRS with excellent intracranial control
- Now s/p 6 cycles carboplatin/pemetrexed with stable/minor response
- Incidental 4 mm right parietal extra-axial lesion (meningioma-appearing, stable throughout)
- Former smoker, 15 pack-years, quit ~10 years ago
- No other significant medical comorbidities documented

ALLERGIES: NKDA

CURRENT MEDICATIONS:
- Folic acid 400 mcg PO daily (pemetrexed supplementation)
- Vitamin B12 1000 mcg IM (last given cycle 6; next dose due per schedule)
- Ondansetron 8 mg PO q8h PRN nausea (currently using infrequently)
- Prochlorperazine 10 mg PO PRN (backup antiemetic; using rarely)
- Omeprazole 20 mg PO daily
- Multivitamin

SOCIAL HISTORY:
Retired schoolteacher. Chinese-American, lives with her husband. Non-smoker (former 15 pack-year history, quit approximately 10 years ago). No alcohol. Highly motivated and engaged patient; supportive family network. Has been researching clinical trial options independently and came to the appointment with written questions.

FAMILY HISTORY:
Mother: lung cancer, deceased age 72. Father: coronary artery disease. No other first-degree relatives with cancer.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
General: Pleasant, alert Chinese-American woman appearing her stated age. No acute distress. Engaged and interactive.
Vitals: BP 118/72, HR 72 bpm regular, RR 16, SpO2 96% on room air, Weight 58.2 kg (up from 56.8 kg at cycle 6; baseline pre-treatment ~61 kg), Temperature 36.8 C.
HEENT: Anicteric sclerae. Oropharynx clear. No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy palpable.
Pulmonary: Mildly decreased breath sounds at left upper lobe. No wheezing or crackles at lung bases. No egophony.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs. No JVD.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly or splenomegaly. No palpable masses.
Extremities: Warm, well-perfused. No pitting edema bilaterally at this visit.
Skin: Mild diffuse hair thinning (Grade 1 alopecia). No acneiform rash currently (previously Grade 1 on osimertinib; resolved). No rash elsewhere.
Neurological: Alert and oriented x4. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Strength 5/5 throughout. Gait steady. No ataxia. No cerebellar signs.
Lymph nodes: No palpable peripheral lymphadenopathy.
ECOG Performance Status: 1.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (Most Recent, Cycle 6 Day 1 and end-of-cycle):
- CBC: WBC 5.8 x10^9/L, Hgb 10.9 g/dL (improved from nadir of 9.1; has been on ESA support), Plt 142 x10^9/L (recovered from nadir of 98), ANC 3.1 x10^9/L
- CMP: Na 138, K 4.1, Cr 0.82 (CrCl ~78 mL/min), ALT 28, AST 22, Alk Phos 74, Total bili 0.5, Albumin 3.8
- CEA: 12.4 ng/mL (down from peak 42 ng/mL at time of progression; prior value ~18 ng/mL at cycle 4 -- trending in the right direction but still elevated above normal)
- Magnesium 0.82 mmol/L (within normal limits)

---

IMAGING / RESTAGING RESULTS (Reviewed at this visit):
CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis with Contrast (approximately 4 weeks prior): Left upper lobe lesion 1.8 x 1.4 cm (stable to minor decrease from 2.1 x 1.7 cm at baseline of this regimen). Right lower lobe nodule 0.5 cm (down from 0.9 cm). Right adrenal nodule stable/slightly smaller at 0.6 cm. No new intrathoracic or abdominal disease. Ongoing post-radiation fibrotic changes in left upper lobe.

MRI Brain with and without contrast (3 months post-SRS): Right frontal SRS site with central necrosis, rim enhancement, overall 0.9 cm -- consistent with post-treatment effect. Left cerebellar lesion: complete radiographic response, no longer visible. No new intracranial metastatic lesions. Right parietal extra-axial meningioma-appearing lesion stable at 4 mm. Excellent intracranial control.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

Ms. Yuen is a 64-year-old woman with EGFR L858R-mutant Stage IIIB NSCLC adenocarcinoma now at the end of her 6-cycle carboplatin/pemetrexed doublet phase, with stable to mildly responding systemic disease and excellent intracranial control post-SRS. Overall she has tolerated this regimen reasonably well given the cumulative toxicities, and her performance status has recovered to ECOG 1.

Key issues addressed today:

1. DISEASE STATUS / RESTAGING: Systemic disease is stable with some minor improvement across all known sites. Brain metastases show excellent response to SRS (right frontal site showing classic post-treatment necrosis changes; left cerebellar lesion completely resolved). CEA trending down (42 --> ~18 --> 12.4 ng/mL), supportive of ongoing disease control, though still elevated. No new sites of disease. I reviewed images personally with the patient today.

2. TRANSITION TO MAINTENANCE / CLINICAL TRIAL DISCUSSION: Given the molecular complexity of her disease -- EGFR L858R retained, T790M acquired resistance mutation, and now high-level MET amplification (copy number ratio 5.8) as a likely co-resistance/bypass pathway -- standard single-agent pemetrexed maintenance is unlikely to provide durable disease control. We had a thorough discussion today regarding the rationale for targeting both the T790M (via osimertinib re-challenge) and MET amplification (via a MET inhibitor) simultaneously.

I have identified a Phase I/II open-label clinical trial investigating tepotinib (a selective MET inhibitor, 500 mg orally daily) in combination with osimertinib (80 mg orally daily) specifically in EGFR-mutant NSCLC patients with MET amplification as an acquired resistance mechanism to prior EGFR TKI therapy. This trial directly addresses her molecular resistance profile and represents a scientifically rational approach. I discussed the trial design, key eligibility criteria, expected toxicities (including peripheral edema and GI toxicity as MET inhibitor class effects; rash, diarrhea, and QTc considerations with osimertinib), and the investigational nature of this combination.

Preliminary eligibility review suggests she likely qualifies: EGFR L858R/T790M confirmed, MET amplification confirmed by tissue NGS (ratio 5.8) and ctDNA concordance, ECOG PS 1, adequate renal function (CrCl ~78 mL/min), and hepatic function within normal limits. I will refer her to our clinical trials team for formal eligibility screening. She is highly interested and motivated; she came with prepared questions and is already familiar with tepotinib from her own research.

3. BRIDGE TO TRIAL: While awaiting formal trial eligibility determination and consent, I recommend transitioning to single-agent pemetrexed maintenance 500 mg/m2 IV q21 days to maintain disease control. Continuation of folic acid and vitamin B12 supplementation. This is a bridging strategy; pemetrexed will be discontinued upon confirmed trial enrollment.

4. ANEMIA: Hemoglobin has improved to 10.9 from nadir of 9.1 g/dL. ESA support ongoing. Will continue and recheck at next visit. Transfusion threshold remains Hgb <8 g/dL or symptomatic anemia.

5. CEA MONITORING: CEA trending down. Will continue monitoring with each cycle.

6. SRS FOLLOW-UP: Next MRI brain scheduled for 6-month post-SRS timepoint. The right frontal lesion will require continued surveillance to distinguish radiation necrosis evolution from possible recurrence; clinical trajectory currently favoring necrosis.

7. SUPPORTIVE CARE: Continue antiemetics PRN as needed. Omeprazole continued. Encourage progressive increase in physical activity as tolerated. Dietary counseling reinforced for continued weight recovery. Referred to survivorship/social work for additional support resources.

Return to clinic: 3 weeks (day 1 of first pemetrexed maintenance cycle) or sooner if symptoms arise. Trial eligibility appointment to be scheduled within the next 5-7 business days.

Time spent with patient and family in counseling and coordination of care: approximately 45 minutes.

[Electronically signed]
[Thoracic Oncology Attending Physician]
